repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance: binary-IV bounded-defier cell accounting; pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1: adjacent up/down response accounting

published_prior_art.cite: Klein2010: local monotonicity departures create MTE/ATE/LATE bias terms, but not this signed envelope interval; https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2009.08.006
HeckmanVytlacil2005: standard LIV/MTE derivative formula under threshold crossing; https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1468-0262.2005.00594.x

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1: finite ordinal bounded-defier polytope; pid_dose_response_iv_v1: open adjacent-rank pasting warning, not continuous-IV signed response envelope

published_prior_art.cite: Marx2024: sharp MTE bounds in monotone latent-index selection, closest missed non-subsuming comparator; https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.02390

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1: generic latent-response LP gap warning, not continuous LIV robustness-curve separation

published_prior_art.cite: Klein2010 plus KennedyLorchSmall2019: bias/inference for monotone LIV curve, not open-neighborhood sharp-set separation or breakdown radius; https://doi.org/10.1111/rssb.12300

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses Marx2024, a J. Econometrics sharp-bounds paper for MTE and linear functionals in the latent-index selection model; it must explicitly separate monotone latent-index sharp MTE bounds from the proposed negative-derivative-envelope sharp set.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Marx2024 as the closest continuous-IV/MTE sharp-bounds comparator and state the epsilon-gap: monotone latent-index sharp bounds versus nonmonotone signed-response envelope with global path compatibility.
Rename the Conjecture 1 phrase '(p'+2f) dz signed measure' to total-variation response measure and explicitly keep p' dz as the signed derivative measure.
Add a concrete two-region, nondegenerate witness template for Conjecture 2 with positive outcome variation and slack inequalities that make the separation open.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around Marx2024, then keep the kernel and proceed if the sharpened epsilon-gap still holds.
